name: Proto-Celtic (reconstructed)
notes: Proto-Celtic (~1200–800 BCE). Reconstructed common ancestor of all Celtic languages. Defining innovation: PIE *p → *ɸ → ∅ (complete p-loss). Other key changes: *gʷ → b, *ē → *ī, *ew → *ow. Preserves PIE labiovelars (*kʷ splits later: Goidelic → /k/, Brythonic → /p/). Incipient lenition of intervocalic stops. Source: Matasović (2009).
